extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN Tom won the 9.7km Jerusalem ITT by one-thousandth of a second over Dennis, averaging over 48kph in extreme heat.
tactical_patterns: Dennis and Froome both adopted extremely high tribars position for aerodynamics on exposed Jerusalem course, revealing equipment tactics.
GONZALEZ Jose finished 9s down unexpectedly from Katusha, suggesting Portuguese climbers may hide TT form better than traditionally expected.
Froome crashed on course recon yet lost only 35s, indicating he paced conservatively rather than risking further incident.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Won by thousandth-second margin sustaining 48kph as big rider in heat, confirming exceptional power-to-drag profile.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: positive: Led for extended period at 48.1kph average, nearly beat world champion despite losing by milliseconds.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Lost 35s after crash on recon but rode cautiously to 19th, clearly prioritizing later mountain stages.
rider_signals.GONZALEZ Jose: positive: Finished 2nd provisionally at 9s down from Katusha, major overperformance from non-TT specialist.
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: Former world champion lost 25s to Dumoulin, signaling decline from peak time-trialing dominance.
